docs.title: Pyletree
docs.text: Pyletree is a simple CLI tool for generating directory tree diagrams.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install pyletree

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
pyletree [ROOT_DIR]

If no directory is provided, the current directory is used:

pyletree

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Usage
  • API

docs.title: API
docs.text: Use the FileTree class programmatically to generate directory trees from Python.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from pyletree import FileTree

tree = FileTree()
print(tree)

for line in tree:
    print(line)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
• root_dir: str | Path = '.' Root directory path
  • dir_only: bool = False Show directories only
  • files_only: bool = False Show files only
  • dirs_first: bool = False List directories before files
  • files_first: bool = False List files before directories
  • no_pipes: bool = False Remove vertical pipes between branches
  • ignore: list[str] | None = None Gitignore-style patterns to ignore
  • filter: list[str] | None = None Gitignore-style patterns to include only
  • use_gitignore: bool | str | Path | list[str | Path] = False Respect .gitignore rules
  • depth_level: int | None = None Limit tree depth
  • path_tree: bool = False Display full paths instead of names
  • text_only: bool = False Plain text mode
  • text_only_indent: int = 2 Indentation spaces for text-only mode
  • file_size: bool = False Show individual file sizes
  • dir_size: bool = False Show cumulative sizes for directories
  • sort_size: None | 'big' | 'small' = None Sort by size
  • reverse: bool = False Reverse alphabetical sort order

docs.title: get_tree() -> str
docs.text: Return the tree as a formatted string.

docs.title: get_dict_tree() -> dict[str, Any]
docs.text: Return the tree as a nested dictionary.

docs.title: get_path(pattern: str) -> list[Path]
docs.text: Search for files or directories matching a pattern.

docs.title: dict(tree)
docs.text: FileTree supports mapping conversion via keys() and __getitem__().

docs.title: Iteration
docs.text: The class is iterable and yields one line of the tree at a time.

docs.title: String conversion
docs.text: str(tree) returns the full tree.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• -h, --help Show help message
  • -v, --version Show version

docs.title: Modes
docs.text:
• -do, --dir-only Show directories only
  • -fo, --files-only Show files only

docs.title: Ordering
docs.text:
• -d, --dirs-first List directories before files
  • -f, --files-first List files before directories
  • -r, --reverse Reverse alphabetical sort order

docs.title: Size
docs.text:
• -fs, --file-size Display individual file sizes
  • -ds, --dir-size Display cumulative directory sizes
  • -b, --big-first Order entries by size (largest first)
  • -s, --small-first Order entries by size (smallest first)

docs.title: Display
docs.text:
• -n, --no-pipes Remove vertical pipes between branches
  • -p, --path-tree Generate a view focused exclusively on full paths
  • -t [N], --text-only [N] Text-only mode: tree in plain text with N spaces indentation (default: 2)

docs.title: Ignoring
docs.text:
• -git [DIR ...], --git [DIR ...] Ignore .git folder and respect .gitignore rules from specific directories or .git folders.
  • -g [DIR_OR_FILE ...], --gitignore [DIR_OR_FILE ...] Respect .gitignore rules from given paths or directories.
  • -i PATTERN [PATTERN ...], --ignore PATTERN [PATTERN ...] Ignore files/directories matching gitignore-style patterns.
  • -fi PATTERN [PATTERN ...], --filter PATTERN [PATTERN ...] Include only files or directories matching gitignore-style patterns.

docs.title: Depth and output
docs.text:
• -dl N, --depth-level N Limit tree depth (must be >= 0)
  • -dt [N], --dict-tree [N] Output the tree as a JSON dictionary. N defines indentation spaces for CLI display only (default: 2). Use 0 for compact output.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
pyletree .
pyletree . -d
pyletree . -fo
pyletree . -dl 2
pyletree . -dt 4
pyletree . -i node_modules dist .git
pyletree . -fi *.py docs/
pyletree . -g
pyletree . -git
pyletree . -n
pyletree . -t 4
pyletree . -fs
pyletree . -ds
pyletree . -b -fs
pyletree . -s -fs
pyletree . -r
pyletree . -p
pyletree src/ -d -fs -dl 3 -i __pycache__
